steps.title: Identify the question type
steps.content: This is a data-supports-claim question. The researchers' conclusion is that there was "a growing interest among CEOs in connecting with more departments" across the years analyzed — so we need a choice describing an upward trend over time.

steps.title: Reason toward the answer
steps.content: An increasing average number of department leaders reporting directly to the CEO across the three periods would directly demonstrate CEOs connecting with more departments over time.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'didn't fluctuate from the 1991–1995 period to the 2001–2008 period': no change would contradict the conclusion of growing interest. ✗
  • Choice B — 'managers...highest in the 1996–2001 period': a middle-period peak is not a rising trend across all three periods, so it doesn't support sustained growth. ✗
  • Choice C — 'department leaders > managers in each of the three periods': this compares two categories at single points; it shows no change over time and therefore doesn't support a growing trend. ✗
That matches choice D — D is correct.
